title: Glassy-Jelly Glazed Ethiopian Chilli Kik Alicha
description: Buttery and sweet Ethiopian-inspired chilli dish featuring a glassy-jelly glaze, perfect for a romantic dinner.
instructions: Preheat your ttukbaegi earthenware cookware or a small Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the ghee or oil and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 5 minutes until it starts to soften.
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chopped taro root, red chilli powder, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add the pitted dates, white balsamic vinegar, and honey. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in 1-2 tablespoons of honey to balance the flavors.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Transfer the kik alicha to a ceramic tagine or a small serving dish.
Drizzle the glassy-jelly glaze (made by cooking the remaining honey and white balsamic vinegar over low heat until thickened) over the top.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro or parsley if desired.
